author: @Tom-ic7hw
text: dont forget ur safety glasses I busted of a 5/8" tap using my lathe being so big I could hammer and punch it out backward a big piece came flying of and cut my nose and yes had on regular glasses with bigger pair safety's on too and yes it hurt and bled

author: @zzzzBadBoyzzzz
text: When I was 1st starting out as a machinist 45 years ago, I watched an old timmer burn a broken tap out with an electrod of some sort on a Bridgeport Milling machine. I know thesedays an EDM machine is the best way to burn them out.

author: @Cooliemasteroz
text: You can sometimes punch out smaller taps without even heating them. I did it with a 5/32” tap once upon a time but I assume the tap that you broke was a fair bit larger and don’t know if it could’ve been done without the welder so that’s a good tip.

author: @noneyabidness6655
text: I bought a go cart hub off evilbay , was about 14 buck with a 3/16 key and grubs already tapped . was for my 30 year old mower they don't sell parts for anymore , well I did find 1 and the rear end differential I found was stupid money , why I just made up a 3/4 solid shaft rear end to drive chain and sprocket instead

author: @davebannister4222
text: use proper machine taps in the first place      not the HSS fucker s         when you sanp em    carbide drills    afer the heat n cool  heat n cool  get the tap hot  not the work piece      if its in thinish steel twat it with a big hammer and shatter the tap      i learned this 45 years ago as an apprentice  shame   the knowledge  dont tricklle down        tho

author: @jaminoes_
text: What happens with the threads when you tap through with this method?

author: @scottwojo7881
text:
Let’s say you break a steel fastener off in an aluminum block....
Set a nut on to the broken bolt and fill the hole with weld.   Once cooler,  un screw the bolt

replies.author: @AtlasReburdened
replies.text: That's all fine if the bolt snapped at or just below the surface, but if the bolt snapped deeper you have to drop in a bit of copper pipe to make up the difference. You can build up a column of weld inside the pipe without melting or marring the aluminum threads.
